items.briefing:
Cybercriminals are abusing shared ChatGPT links to host fake outage pages that trick users into downloading malware — be cautious clicking any ChatGPT links shared via email or messaging apps, even from known contacts. Separately, a new report highlights that organisations are taking longer than ever to disclose data breaches after discovery, meaning your personal information may be compromised well before you're notified — it's worth regularly checking haveibeenpwned.com to see if your details have appeared in known breaches.

**Practical tip:** If a ChatGPT or AI tool link looks unusual or asks you to download anything, don't proceed — report it to your IT team.

featured.title: Dragos acquires Phosphorus to widen OT security reach
featured.quote: Critical infrastructure operators gain better visibility into operational technology security threats.
